meta_description: Precision EDM(Electrical Discharge Machining) services at JLCCNC for engineers and businesses, offering high-quality machining with fast turnaround.

sections.heading: EDM(Electrical Discharge Machining)
sections.text: EDM(Electrical Discharge Machining) Electrical Discharge Machining (EDM) is based on a unique process where electrical sparks are used to remove material from a workpiece, resulting in the desired shape or design. Get Instant Quote

sections.heading: Materials Suitable for EDM
sections.text: Materials Suitable for EDM EDM (Electrical Discharge Machining) is suitable for conductive materials, such as metals and alloys, including steel, titanium, copper, and tungsten carbide. The material must be electrically conductive to facilitate the spark erosion process.Here are the materials JLCCNC can provide for EDM. Brass Steel Aluminum See All Materials >

sections.heading: EDM Design Guidelines
sections.text: EDM Design Guidelines Features Description Max Build Size (Conventional EDM) 400*300*300mm Max Build Size (Wire EDM) 300*300*400mm Minimum Build Size (Conventional & Wire EDM) Ø0.5 Recommend Size (Conventional & Wire EDM) ±0.01mm Recommend Accuracy (Conventional & Wire EDM) ±0.05mm

sections.heading: How Is EDM Machine Working?
sections.text: How Is EDM Machine Working? An EDM (Electrical Discharge Machining) machine works by using electrical sparks to erode material from a conductive workpiece, typically in the presence of a dielectric fluid, allowing for precise machining of complex shapes and hard materials.
